verses.text: When Ahaziah dies, his mother Athaliah murders all the royal seed to seize the throne — but baby Joash is hidden by his aunt for six years. The line of David hangs by a thread, kept alive in secret. God preserves His promise even when one wicked woman would destroy it.

When Ahaziah dies, his mother Athaliah murders all the royal seed to seize the throne — but baby Joash is hidden by his aunt for six years. The line of David hangs by a thread, kept alive in secret. God preserves His promise even when one wicked woman would destroy it.

When Ahaziah dies, his mother Athaliah murders all the royal seed to seize the throne — but baby Joash is hidden by his aunt for six years. The line of David hangs by a thread, kept alive in secret. God preserves His promise even when one wicked woman would destroy it.

After six years the priest Jehoiada gathers the captains, shows them the hidden king's son, and arms them to guard him. Faith waits for the right moment, then acts with courage and care. God's purposes are advanced by patient planning as well as by patient hope.

After six years the priest Jehoiada gathers the captains, shows them the hidden king's son, and arms them to guard him. Faith waits for the right moment, then acts with courage and care. God's purposes are advanced by patient planning as well as by patient hope.

They bring out the boy, crown him, give him the testimony, anoint him, and cry, 'God save the king.' The rightful heir is enthroned at last. What tyranny tried to erase, God brings forth in His time, to the joy of His people.

They bring out the boy, crown him, give him the testimony, anoint him, and cry, 'God save the king.' The rightful heir is enthroned at last. What tyranny tried to erase, God brings forth in His time, to the joy of His people.

Athaliah hears the noise, cries 'Treason!', and is led out and slain. The usurper who killed to gain a throne loses both it and her life. Power seized by murder cannot stand against the God who keeps His covenant with David.

Jehoiada makes a covenant that they should be the LORD's people, and the people tear down Baal's temple, altars, and images, killing his priest. The coup is also a reformation. True renewal of a nation casts down its idols, not merely its tyrants.

Jehoiada makes a covenant that they should be the LORD's people, and the people tear down Baal's temple, altars, and images, killing his priest. The coup is also a reformation. True renewal of a nation casts down its idols, not merely its tyrants.

Joash is set on the throne, the city is quiet, and the seven-year-old king begins to reign. After bloodshed comes peace. God brings His preserved heir to the throne, and the land that groaned under Athaliah rejoices.
